title: VR Title Metadata Not Propagated by Interactive Engine

comment:
PRE-REVIEW: CR-091-ADD-001-VAR-001 v0.1 — ONE DEFICIENCY. Request updates.

FRONTMATTER:
- title: "VR Title Metadata Not Propagated by Interactive Engine" — present, non-empty.
- revision_summary: "Initial draft" — acceptable for v0.1 of self-authorizing executable document per SOP-002 Section 8.

STRUCTURE: 9 sections present (Variance Identification, Detailed Description, Root Cause, Variance Type, Impact Assessment, Proposed Resolution, Resolution Work, VAR Closure, References).

SECTION-BY-SECTION ASSESSMENT:

Section 1 (Variance Identification): Parent document CR-091-ADD-001, failed item EI-2, VAR Type 2. All three fields populated. Correct.

Section 2 (Detailed Description): Two deficiencies clearly described with expected vs. actual framing. The description accurately characterizes both the title propagation gap and the SOP-004/TEMPLATE-VR alignment discrepancy. I confirm the alignment discrepancy: I approved TEMPLATE-VR v2.1 (CR-098, items 8-9) which removed the Signature section, but SOP-004 Section 9C.4 was not updated concurrently.

Section 3 (Root Cause): Two independent root causes identified. System Error for title propagation (CLI initialization gap) and Documentation Error for SOP/template misalignment. Both are accurately characterized.

Section 4 (Variance Type): "System Error and Documentation Error" — consistent with Section 3. Correct.

Section 5 (Impact Assessment): Low impact with five supporting points. The assessment that VR evidence is complete, valid, and traceable is consistent with my own findings from the prior review. The per-response attribution point is valid — interactive VRs provide per-entry author/timestamp attribution that is arguably stronger than a single end-of-document signature.

Section 6 (Proposed Resolution): Deferred to future CR. Two specific corrective actions identified: (1) fix CLI title propagation, (2) align SOP-004 Section 9C.4 with TEMPLATE-VR v5. Clear and actionable.

Section 7 (Resolution Work): States resolution deferred, no execution work required. Appropriate for a Type 2 VAR where pre-approval is sufficient to unblock parent.

Section 8 (VAR Closure): Execution-time placeholders. Correct for pre-approval stage.

Section 9 (References): Lists SOP-004, CR-091-ADD-001, CR-091-ADD-001-VR-001, CR-098, TEMPLATE-VR v3.0, and SOP-004 Section 9C.4. Comprehensive and accurate.

TYPE 2 CLASSIFICATION: Appropriate. Per SOP-004 Section 9A.2, Type 2 applies when "Impact is contained; parent's core objectives are met; VAR prevents follow-up from falling off radar." The impact assessment supports this: VR evidence is complete, parent objective met, deficiencies are traceable and documented for future correction.

PLACEHOLDER CHECK: No TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX. Square-bracket placeholders in Sections 7 and 8 are execution-time editable fields per template convention.
FORMATTING: Consistent throughout. Section numbering sequential (1-9).
TYPOS/ERRORS: None found.

---

DEFICIENCY: Missing Scope Handoff section.

SOP-004 Section 9A.5 requires: "When a VAR is created from a failed execution item, it must explicitly specify: 1. What was successfully accomplished in the parent (before the variance) 2. What the VAR absorbs (takes responsibility for) 3. Confirmation that no scope items were lost in the handoff."

The VAR does not contain a Scope Handoff section. The substance is partially present — Section 2 notes that EI-2 execution succeeded with all verification steps passing, and Section 5 confirms the parent's core objective was met — but the formal three-item structure required by SOP-004 Section 9A.5 is absent. This must be added as a labeled section with all three items explicitly addressed.

RECOMMENDATION: Add a Scope Handoff section (between Impact Assessment and Proposed Resolution, or between Proposed Resolution and Resolution Work) with the three required items per SOP-004 Section 9A.5. After correction, re-route for pre-review.

PRE-REVIEW (re-review): CR-091-ADD-001-VAR-001 v0.1 — COMPLIANT. Recommend proceeding to pre-approval.

PRIOR DEFICIENCY RESOLUTION:
The single deficiency from my prior review (missing Scope Handoff section per SOP-004 Section 9A.5) has been corrected. Section 5 (Scope Handoff) now contains all three required items:
1. What was successfully accomplished in parent — all 4 EIs completed, VR authored interactively with 4 verification steps, all Pass.
2. What this VAR absorbs — documentation and future correction of two deficiencies: empty title field (CLI gap), SOP-004/TEMPLATE-VR discrepancy (Signature requirement).
3. No scope items lost — all parent EIs completed, VR evidence complete and traceable, no scope reduction.
All three items are substantive and accurately characterize the scope relationship.

FULL DOCUMENT RE-VERIFICATION:

FRONTMATTER:
- title: "VR Title Metadata Not Propagated by Interactive Engine" — present, non-empty.
- revision_summary: "Initial draft" — acceptable for v0.1 of self-authorizing executable document per SOP-002 Section 8.

STRUCTURE: 10 sections present (Variance Identification, Detailed Description, Root Cause, Variance Type, Scope Handoff, Impact Assessment, Proposed Resolution, Resolution Work, VAR Closure, References). All required VAR sections per SOP-004 Section 9A.3 present, plus the Scope Handoff per Section 9A.5. Section numbering sequential (1-10).

CONTENT VERIFICATION (sections unchanged from prior review):
- Section 1: Parent CR-091-ADD-001, failed item EI-2, VAR Type 2. Correct.
- Section 2: Two deficiencies accurately described with expected vs. actual. Confirmed.
- Section 3: System Error and Documentation Error root causes. Accurately characterized.
- Section 4: Variance type consistent with root cause analysis.
- Section 6: Low impact with five supporting points. Consistent with my own review findings.
- Section 7: Resolution deferred to future CR. Two specific corrective actions. Clear and actionable.
- Section 8: Resolution deferred, no execution work. Appropriate for Type 2 VAR.
- Section 9: Execution-time placeholders. Correct for pre-approval stage.
- Section 10: All relevant documents referenced.

TYPE 2 CLASSIFICATION: Confirmed appropriate per SOP-004 Section 9A.2.
PLACEHOLDER CHECK: No TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX found.
FORMATTING: Consistent throughout.
TYPOS/ERRORS: None found.
